Your Healing Kitchen
Natural healing · Fourth trimester
Herbal tea • Postpartum recovery • Digestion & milk

Fennel Tea

A sweet, soothing seed tea for digestion and milk supply.
Makes 1 cup
Time 10 min
Caffeine none
For you · after meals
When to drink After meals.

Why this heals you

  • Fennel — soothes digestion, eases bloating tradition
  • The classic milk-support (galactagogue) tea tradition
  • Naturally sweet & gentle tradition
  • Caffeine-free tradition
Ingredients
1 tspFennel seeds (lightly crushed)
1 cupJust-boiled water
optionalA little honey (to taste)
Method
1
Crush

Lightly crush 1 tsp fennel seeds.

2
Steep

Pour over a cup of just-boiled water; cover and steep 5–10 min.

3
Sip

Strain and sip warm.

Just for you, Mum

Sweet and settling — a lovely after-dinner cup that also gives your milk a gentle nudge.

Good to know
BestAfter meals, warm.
Pairs withCaraway or a few cumin seeds for a stronger digestive brew.
Why we say this
Fennel as a digestive and galactagogue is well-established tradition (and a common postpartum tea); labelled tradition.
Main Meals
Snacks
Drinks
Desserts
Food, not medical advice · non-diagnostic · made for this season of motherhood
